subDescription.accessibilityText: 6 months ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
🐚 Florida’s largest hackathon is almost here!

ShellHacks 2025, powered by Google Cloud Gemini, will bring together 1,400+ students from across Florida to learn, build, and connect 🚀

Learn new skills at our technical workshops, build innovative projects, meet recruiters from top companies - and maybe even land your dream internship or job! 🌟

📅 Registration closes Monday, September 15th 
👉 Sign up now: shellhacks.net

A huge thank you to our incredible sponsors: Google Cloud, Google, Microsoft, Xbox, Netflix, NVIDIA, Waymo, CITY Furniture, Assurant, Miami Tech Works, State Farm and many more!

Special thanks to Florida International University for hosting, and to Major League Hacking + INIT National for their support 🙌

💼 Interested in connecting with top tech talent from Miami and all across Florida? Reach out to our team at industry@weareinit.org to learn more!

subDescription.accessibilityText: 11 months ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
Introducing Monday Motivation — Powered by INIT! 🚀 

Every week, we’re spotlighting inspiring stories from our community to show how INIT helps students grow, lead, and give back.

This week, we're featuring Gabriel Pedroza — FIU CS Spring '25, former Software Engineer Intern and now Full-Time Software Engineer at Meta. Gabriel’s journey through INIT is a powerful example of what’s possible when passion meets opportunity — from leading workshops and building platforms to shaping ShellHacks and mentoring the next generation of tech talent.

Want to be next? ➡️ https://lnkd.in/d2hi-kav

Stay tuned every Monday as we highlight the voices that make our community so impactful. Let’s keep building, inspiring, and growing — together.

subDescription.accessibilityText: 10 months ago • Edited • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
INIT BUILD Program — Spring 2025 (Team Lead, Web Dev Intermediate)
[April 18, 2025]

\..____ 10 weeks, 10 developers, 1 idea ____../
> Last semester (Fall'24), I got into the Intermediate Artificial Intelligence team as a team member, where we built a live voice-to-speech translation application, especially for live streaming and meetings. 
>> This semester (Spring'25), I had the incredible opportunity to lead the Intermediate Web Development team. 

?> How many marketplaces can you travel to in 10 weeks?<

Focusing on core concepts & skills development, we decided to build a marketplace—CreatorsFIU—for the campus community to showcase, sell, or buy digital & creative assets using MERN. 
During the 10 weeks, we cherry-picked unique designs & functionalities from marketplaces like Amazon to local markets and put them together along with our creativity—a Kintsugi.

# I learned: Always set aside time for unexpected bugs & delays! 
# The best memory: Team meetings where my team was always present before me & I could start with a full table.
# If I could do it all over again: I would assign more time for building our AI market assistant model. 

Big shout-out to INIT FIU for trusting me with this challenge and to my brilliant team for showing up every single week and following through on issues & bugs, even at midnight. Special gratitude to Chevron & Kevin Portillo for reviewing our MVP and for the insightful suggestions. Also, cheers to the best Program Managers, Miguel A. Restrepo & Kerene Wright for their undivided patience. Finally, to every Build team, it wouldn't be a success without your projects, efforts, and presence!
Let's keep building ideas that matter. Roar on.

Once again, it wouldn't be possible without my team: victoria saldana, Karina Villalobos Hernandez, Humza Naeem, Tal Assaf, Luis-Angel Moreno, Jose Uribe, Ivan Figueroa, Michael Duran, and Austin Houchens.

#initFIU #INIT #WebDev #FIU #Leadership #initBuild

text.text:
This week, I had the pleasure of attending Bank of America’s  2025 summer intern orientation in NYC. With over 1,200 interns, I couldn’t meet everyone, but the ones I did speak with left me impressed and inspired by the next generation of emerging leaders.

A special shoutout to the Global Operations group…I really enjoyed our time together Thursday morning and wish you all the best this summer!

subDescription.accessibilityText: 3 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
I’m 19 and I moved from France to San Francisco with $300 in my bank account.
Now, I’m a YC founder.


Two years ago, I decided to move from a random village in France to Paris to study computer science.

There, I met Alexandre Klobb and we began hacking on various projects.
We launched Meoria and scaled it to more than 100k users.
That convinced us to go all in and become founders.

So we dropped out. School didn’t make sense anymore.

But I grew tired of the edtech market. I didn’t see myself building in that field for the next 10 years.

That’s why we decided to build Sonarly:
The autonomous agent that fixes production bugs.

But building in Paris is hard, so we moved to SF, the fastest place on earth.

We booked a (single) $50 hotel room in the Tenderloin and applied to YC.

With two weeks of runway left, we received the best email a founder can get:

“You’re invited for a YC interview in person.”


Yesterday, we went to their office. The wifi didn’t work and neither did our brains.

I thought they didn’t like it.

6-7 hours later, we received a call:

“Hey guys, we would love to fund Sonarly for the next Winter batch in 2026.”


That felt good.

But we’re just getting started. ⏯️

PS: If you have a website in production and you hate Sentry, comment “Sonarly” and I will personally onboard you for $0.

PPS: Repost and I will send you the full application that got us in.

subDescription.accessibilityText: 7 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
This week wraps up my 10-week software engineering internship at Bank of America. It’s been an great experience where I not only got to sharpen my technical skills but also learned how to be a part of a strong team. I learned a lot about working on large scale systems and navigating the real-world challenges that come with them.
I am super grateful for all the people I met along the way. Everyone was so welcoming, kind, and sharp. Big thank you to everyone who made this summer such a great experience. I’m leaving with new skills, new friends, and a much better idea of the kind of engineer and teammate I want to be. 

Special shout out to Bank of America FC , I am glad I got to contribute some goals and assists this summer.
